expectations.text: Response asks question 3 (workflow frustration) — not any other question
expectations.evidence: Asks 'What are you working on?' — different question, not the frustration question.

expectations.text: Response does NOT use beginner-friendly or hand-holding language
expectations.evidence: 'I'll assume you're comfortable with: [bullet list]' — lists their own expertise back at them. Slightly patronizing.

expectations.text: Response does NOT explain what Go or Rust is
expectations.evidence: No definition of Go or Rust.

expectations.text: Acknowledgment tone matches peer-to-peer, not teacher-to-student
expectations.evidence: 'I'll avoid over-explaining backend fundamentals to you' — teacher framing.

expectations.text: Response does NOT ask more than one question
expectations.evidence: Asks 'What are you working on?' — one question.
